# Christian Communities Walkthrough — Draft

**Status**: drafting — investigation phase. Sections are question-led; thesis + flow not yet committed.

**Audience**: yourself (private investigation doc; gets distilled into a public walkthrough page once the flow solidifies)

**Sibling**: [muslim-walkthrough-draft.md](./muslim-walkthrough-draft.md)

---

## How to use this doc

Each numbered section below is a question we're trying to answer. Each contains:
- The question
- What the data says (tables / lists)
- Interpretation + caveats
- Open follow-ups

When the picture becomes clear, the sections that fit a narrative get distilled into the walkthrough page. Sections that don't fit get archived or moved to a "what we ruled out" appendix.

---

## Provisional thesis (will evolve)

> Within Kerala's Christian electorate, sub-rite identity is a **second-order variable** — religion population share predicts UDF *level* better than sub-rite does, but sub-rite tracks **historical alignment** and **multi-cycle trajectory**. Latin Catholic was structurally LDF-leaning through 2011–2021 then snapped back to UDF in 2026; Syro-Malabar has been UDF's most reliable Catholic base; Indian Orthodox is the perennial swing; CSI cohort is really a Trivandrum-geographic cluster, not a denomination effect.

This needs iteration. Sections below are organised to test and refine it.

---

## §1 — What's each denomination's **historical** alliance preference?

> The Sprint 2 report focused on 2021→2026 swings. But to read 2026 properly we need each cohort's **default alignment** — what they look like over the past 4 cycles, not just one snapshot.

### Multi-cycle UDF / LDF / NDA mean per Christian cohort

Generated by `scripts/analyze-christian-cohort-detail.ts`.

#### Latin Catholic (n=28)

| Year | UDF | LDF | NDA | Wins (U/L/N) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|
| 2011 | 44.2 | 49.4 | 4.5 | 9/19/0 |
| 2016 | 36.2 | 47.8 | 14.3 | 4/24/0 |
| 2021 | 36.7 | 47.1 | 13.2 | 4/24/0 |
| **2026** | **45.6** | **39.7** | **13.7** | **21/6/1** |

**Reading**: Latin Catholic was a **structurally LDF-leaning cohort** through 2011–2021. The 2026 +9pp UDF swing isn't a directional shift — it's a **return to mid-1990s/2011-era competitive territory** plus a small premium. UDF won only 4/28 Latin Catholic ACs in both 2016 and 2021; now wins 21/28.

#### Syro-Malabar (n=31)

| Year | UDF | LDF | NDA | Wins (U/L/N) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|
| 2011 | 48.7 | 44.3 | 5.1 | 21/10/0 |
| 2016 | 40.5 | 40.1 | 16.4 | 14/16/0 |
| 2021 | 40.2 | 43.8 | 11.8 | 13/18/0 |
| **2026** | **48.4** | **35.8** | **14.7** | **26/5/0** |

**Reading**: Syro-Malabar is the **historically UDF-leaning Catholic cohort** (won 21/31 in 2011). LDF clawed it close in 2016/2021 but didn't take it. 2026 restored UDF dominance. The +8.2pp swing is a return to 2011-era levels.

#### Indian Orthodox (n=10)

| Year | UDF | LDF | NDA | Wins (U/L/N) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|
| 2011 | 47.9 | 45.4 | 5.0 | 5/5/0 |
| 2016 | 38.5 | 43.1 | 17.1 | 3/7/0 |
| 2021 | 37.6 | 44.4 | 16.5 | 2/8/0 |
| **2026** | **44.8** | **37.6** | **16.9** | **5/4/1** |

**Reading**: Orthodox is **the perennial 50/50 cohort**. Even in UDF's worst year (2021), the average margin was just 7pp. Even in UDF's best year (2026), wins split 5/4/1. NDA presence (~17%) is consistently the highest of all Christian Catholic-family cohorts.

#### Jacobite Syrian (n=3)

| Year | UDF | LDF | NDA | Wins (U/L/N) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|
| 2011 | 49.5 | 43.2 | 4.4 | 3/0/0 |
| 2016 | 42.4 | 45.6 | 10.8 | 2/1/0 |
| 2021 | 42.7 | 39.5 | 5.0 | 1/2/0 |
| **2026** | **52.1** | **33.7** | **13.3** | **3/0/0** |

**Reading**: Small n=3. Jacobite was UDF-leaning historically but variable. 2026 NDA share jumped from 5 to 13 (driven by Twenty 20 — see §3). 100% UDF win rate in 2026.

#### Marthoma (n=2)

| Year | UDF | LDF | NDA | Wins (U/L/N) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|
| 2011 | 45.9 | 46.2 | 6.9 | 1/1/0 |
| 2016 | 34.4 | 42.3 | 22.5 | 0/2/0 |
| 2021 | 37.4 | 43.8 | 16.7 | 0/2/0 |
| **2026** | **44.5** | **36.7** | **18.5** | **2/0/0** |

**Reading**: n=2 — Aranmula + Ranni — treat as suggestive. Marthoma ACs went LDF in 2016 and 2021, flipped to UDF in 2026. Followed Latin Catholic's pattern.

#### CSI (n=5)

| Year | UDF | LDF | NDA | Wins (U/L/N) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|
| 2011 | 36.2 | 46.6 | 15.5 | 3/2/0 |
| 2016 | 31.0 | 40.1 | 28.1 | 1/3/1 |
| 2021 | 33.3 | 43.9 | 21.4 | 1/4/0 |
| **2026** | **35.4** | **38.1** | **25.5** | **2/2/1** |

**Reading**: CSI cohort has been **flat UDF** across all four cycles (31–36% range). LDF dominant historically; 2026 narrowed gap but didn't flip. NDA share is consistently 21–28% — highest of any Christian cohort. The 1 NDA win is Nemom (2016 + 2026). See §2 — this cohort is mostly Trivandrum-district.

#### Below-threshold residual (n=61)

| Year | UDF | LDF | NDA | Wins (U/L/N) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|
| 2011 | 46.3 | 43.5 | 6.9 | 30/31/0 |
| 2016 | 40.9 | 43.7 | 13.4 | 24/37/0 |
| 2021 | 40.8 | 45.7 | 11.4 | 20/41/0 |
| **2026** | **47.1** | **38.2** | **13.5** | **43/18/0** |

**Reading**: The baseline for "no consequential Christian sub-rite" ACs (Muslim-belt + Hindu-majority interior). Their +6.3pp swing is the **anti-incumbency signal** the cohort comparisons need to net out against. Cohorts that beat +6.3 are responding to Christian-specific cues; cohorts that match or under-perform aren't.

### Re-reading the 2026 swing against baseline

| Cohort | Δ UDF | vs +6.3 baseline |
|---|---:|---|
| Jacobite | +9.4 | +3.1 above |
| Latin Catholic | +9.0 | +2.7 above |
| Syro-Malabar | +8.2 | +1.9 above |
| Indian Orthodox | +7.3 | +1.0 above |
| Marthoma | +7.1 | +0.8 above |
| CSI | +2.2 | **−4.1 below** |

Every Christian cohort except CSI beat or matched the statewide-baseline UDF swing. **CSI is the only Christian cohort that swung *less* than the baseline** — that's the anomaly worth investigating in §2.

### Open follow-ups for §1

- [ ] Cross-cycle UDF trend chart per cohort for the walkthrough page
- [ ] Compute Δ UDF cohort R² across multi-cycle data (does sub-rite identity predict swing direction consistently across cycles, or only in 2026?)

---

## §2 — What's special about the CSI cohort? (the LDF outlier)

> CSI cohort barely moved (+2.2pp) while every other Christian cohort gained +7-9pp. What's going on?

### Which ACs?

The 5 CSI-cohort ACs:

| AC | District | Christian % | 2021 winner | 2026 winner |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| Nedumangad | Thiruvananthapuram | 13.5% | LDF | LDF |
| Nemom | Thiruvananthapuram | 16.8% | NDA | NDA |
| Parassala | Thiruvananthapuram | 45.1% | LDF | LDF |
| Kattakkada | Thiruvananthapuram | 25.4% | LDF | UDF |
| Kovalam | Thiruvananthapuram | 16.8% | UDF | UDF |

**All 5 are in Trivandrum district.** This is a **falsifier landing**: the "CSI cohort" isn't a denomination effect — it's a Trivandrum-district cluster where CSI happens to be the dominant Christian sub-rite *because the absolute Christian count is small enough that a few CSI parishes outnumber Latin/Catholic parishes*.

### Reframe

The CSI cohort is actually two things at once:
1. **Trivandrum-district interior ACs** (Nedumangad, Parassala, Kattakkada) where Christian population is 13–45% but no Catholic sub-rite dominates the limited Christian parish count
2. **BJP-competitive Trivandrum ACs** (Nemom, Kovalam, Kazhakkoottam-style) where NDA share is structurally 25%+

The +2.2pp UDF swing is then **the Trivandrum-interior swing**, not the CSI-denomination swing. CSI Christians may behave any way — we can't tell from this cohort because the cohort label is geographically confounded.

### Implication for the walkthrough

This finding doesn't go in as "CSI is LDF-leaning". It goes in as: **"The Christian-sub-rite-cohort framework breaks down in Trivandrum district, where Christian populations are smaller and the dominant sub-rite signal mixes Christian-community effects with Trivandrum-political effects (BJP strength, LDF interior strength)."**

### Open follow-ups for §2

- [ ] Compare Trivandrum-district below-threshold ACs (no CSI cohort) to the 5 CSI cohort ACs. If they look similar, the cohort effect is purely district.
- [ ] Try a wider Christian threshold (e.g., 30%) — does any CSI cohort survive? Probably not.
- [ ] Surface this finding in the walkthrough — it's an honest "what the data can't tell us" caveat.

---

## §3 — Twenty 20 is Jacobite-Syrian-led. Did Jacobite seats get attention?

> Twenty 20 is the Kitex-Garments-backed local party founded by Sabu M Jacob, a Jacobite Syrian. NDA partner in 2026. Did they cluster in Jacobite-cohort ACs?

### Where Twenty 20 contested in 2026 (19 ACs, all NDA-aligned)

| Christian cohort | Twenty 20 ACs contested | Max share | Median share |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Latin Catholic | 7 | Kodungallur 16.3% | ~7% |
| Syro-Malabar | 8 | Thripunithura 19.2% | ~10% |
| Indian Orthodox | 1 (Pathanapuram) | 5.1% | 5.1% |
| Jacobite Syrian | 2 (Kunnathunad 25.1%, Piravom 9.8%) | 25.1% | 17% |
| Marthoma | 1 (Ranni) | 14.6% | 14.6% |
| Below threshold | 0 | — | — |

### Reading

Twenty 20's geographic strategy is **Ernakulam-district-centred**, not Jacobite-denomination-centred. Their stronghold is Kunnathunad (Kitex's home, where Sabu Jacob lives — the only Jacobite-cohort AC they got >25% in). The 19 ACs contested are concentrated around **Kunnathunad and adjacent ACs in Ernakulam, Kottayam, Idukki, Thrissur**. They contested across Christian cohorts but heavily in Ernakulam's geographic vicinity.

So:
- ❌ **No** — Twenty 20 didn't preferentially target Jacobite Syrian cohort ACs as a denomination strategy
- ✅ **Yes** — Twenty 20 is geographically concentrated where Christians (of various sub-rites) are demographically significant
- ⚠️ Kunnathunad 25% Twenty 20 + Piravom 10% Twenty 20 IS what pushed the Jacobite cohort's 2026 NDA share from 5% (2021) to 13%

### Open follow-ups for §3

- [ ] List the 19 Twenty 20 ACs with their NDA share — confirm Kunnathunad as the spike
- [ ] Did Twenty 20 contest in Kunnathunad in 2021? Track their growth.
- [ ] Is Twenty 20's effect: (a) pulling NDA share off BJP, or (b) winning votes that would otherwise have been UDF/LDF? Need candidate-level breakdown.

---

## §4 — Where did the BJP/NDA do well in Christian-cohort ACs?

> The party walkthroughs note BJP's targeted gains. Were the targets Christian-area? Which sub-rites?

### NDA mean share per Christian cohort (2021 → 2026)

| Cohort | NDA 2021 | NDA 2026 | Δ NDA | NDA wins 2026 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Latin Catholic | 13.2 | 13.7 | +0.5 | 1 (Kazhakkoottam) |
| Syro-Malabar | 11.8 | 14.7 | +2.9 | 0 |
| Indian Orthodox | 16.5 | 16.9 | +0.4 | 1 (TBD — likely Ranni or Konni adjacent) |
| Jacobite | 5.0 | 13.3 | **+8.3** | 0 (Twenty 20 effect) |
| Marthoma | 16.7 | 18.5 | +1.8 | 0 |
| **CSI** | **21.4** | **25.5** | **+4.2** | **1 (Nemom)** |
| Below threshold | 11.4 | 13.5 | +2.1 | 0 (BJP elsewhere — Hindu seats) |

### Reading

- **NDA's only Christian-cohort wins are in the CSI cohort (Nemom)** and Latin Catholic cohort (Kazhakkoottam). Both are Trivandrum-district. NDA's Christian-area wins are entirely a **Trivandrum phenomenon**.
- **Jacobite Syrian NDA share jumped +8.3pp** — almost entirely the Twenty 20 effect (Kunnathunad alone).
- **Syro-Malabar NDA share crept up +2.9pp**, slightly above baseline (+2.1pp). Modest, no wins.
- **Latin Catholic NDA was essentially flat** — community appears resistant to NDA absorption.

### Open follow-ups for §4

- [ ] BJP party-share (vs NDA total) per cohort — separate out Twenty 20 effect to see "pure BJP" picture
- [ ] Multi-cycle NDA trend per cohort — is Christian-area NDA growth a steady trend or 2016-era one-time?

---

## §5 — Is "Latin Catholics don't support BJP" supported by the data?

> Common-knowledge claim. Test it.

### Mean BJP party-share 2026 per cohort (excluding alliance partners, just BJP itself)

| Cohort | n | Mean BJP 2026 |
|---|---:|---:|
| **Latin Catholic** | 28 | **9.6%** |
| Syro-Malabar | 31 | 10.5% |
| Indian Orthodox | 10 | 13.2% |
| Below threshold | 61 | 12.2% |

Latin Catholic has the **lowest BJP party-share** among Christian cohorts. The common-knowledge claim is **directionally correct**.

### But — within Latin Catholic, NDA share varies a lot

Highest NDA in Latin Catholic cohort (all Trivandrum/Ernakulam):
- Kazhakkoottam 35.7% (TVM — NDA win)
- Attingal 30.8% (TVM)
- Thiruvananthapuram 29.4% (TVM)
- Chirayinkeezhu 22.5% (TVM)
- Ernakulam 18.0% (Kochi)

Lowest NDA in Latin Catholic cohort (all north Kerala):
- Trikaripur 4.6% (Kasaragod)
- Thiruvambadi 4.7% (Kozhikode)
- Payyannur 5.4% (Kannur)
- Peravoor 5.5% (Kannur)
- Irikkur 6.4% (Kannur)

### Reading

The Latin Catholic cohort splits **geographically** on BJP:
- **South coastal Latin (Trivandrum / Kollam coast)**: BJP gets 20-35% — meaningful presence
- **Central coastal Latin (Alappuzha / Kochi)**: BJP gets 9-18%
- **North Kerala Latin (Kannur / Kasaragod / Kozhikode)**: BJP gets 0-6% — essentially absent

So the more honest framing is: **"Latin Catholics in north Kerala don't support BJP. Latin Catholics in Trivandrum split between BJP and UDF."** The community isn't homogeneous; the south coast has structural BJP presence.

### Open follow-ups for §5

- [ ] Repeat the within-cohort geographic split for Syro-Malabar and Orthodox — does the same north/south pattern appear?
- [ ] Is the south-coast BJP strength a Latin Catholic phenomenon or a generic-coastal-TVM phenomenon? Compare with non-Christian-cohort Trivandrum ACs.

---

## §6 — Does UDF allocate Kerala Congress / KEC seats by denomination?

> UDF includes INC + Kerala Congress factions (KEC, KC-Jacob, KC-B) + IUML + RSP + smaller allies. Is the Kerala-Congress allocation denomination-aligned?

### UDF candidate party per Christian cohort (2026)

| Cohort | n | INC | Kerala Congress | KC-Jacob | KEC + KC-J % | IUML | Other |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Latin Catholic | 28 | 18 | 2 | 0 | 7% | 2 | 6 |
| Syro-Malabar | 31 | 25 | 4 | 0 | 13% | 1 | 1 |
| Indian Orthodox | 10 | 9 | 1 | 0 | 10% | 0 | 0 |
| Jacobite | 3 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 67% | 0 | 0 |
| Marthoma | 2 | 2 | 0 | 0 | 0% | 0 | 0 |
| CSI | 5 | 5 | 0 | 0 | 0% | 0 | 0 |
| Below threshold | 61 | 32 | 0 | 0 | 0% | 23 | 6 |

### Reading

- **INC is the default UDF face everywhere** — 18/28 in Latin, 25/31 in SM, 5/5 in CSI, 32/61 in residual.
- **Kerala Congress (the main faction) is allocated most in Syro-Malabar cohort** (4 of 31, ~13%). Matches the historical KEC base in Pala/Kanjirapally/Kottayam Syro-Malabar territory.
- **Latin Catholic cohort has notably fewer KEC seats** (2/28, 7%) than Syro-Malabar. INC handles most Latin Catholic ACs directly.
- **Jacobite cohort (n=3) gets the most denomination-matched allocation**: KEC + KC-Jacob = 2 of 3. KC-Jacob is the faction historically led by Anoop Jacob, a Jacobite-aligned politician.
- **CSI + Marthoma cohorts get 100% INC.** No Kerala Congress allocation. These are Protestant cohorts; KEC's base is Catholic.

### The cleanest finding here:

**UDF's Kerala Congress allocation is *Catholic-aligned*, not pan-Christian.** Within Catholics, Syro-Malabar gets the larger share. Protestant Christian cohorts (CSI, Marthoma) get INC-only candidates. This mirrors the historical Catholic-faction politics of Kerala Congress.

### Open follow-ups for §6

- [ ] Compute KEC vote share per cohort (not candidate count) — does KEC actually win more seats in SM cohort?
- [ ] Multi-cycle: when was KEC's Latin Catholic allocation last >10%? Has KEC's Catholic base narrowed to Syro-Malabar over time?
- [ ] KC-Jacob in Piravom (Jacobite cohort) — is this a recent factional alignment or longstanding?

---

## §7 — Three sub-rite profiles, reframed

> Sprint 2 framed this as "Catholics moved together, Orthodox stayed competitive, CSI barely moved". The deep-dive reframes it.

### Revised three profiles

After §1-§6 above:

1. **UDF-historically-aligned cohorts that returned to UDF in 2026**:
   - Syro-Malabar (always UDF-leaning, +8.2 swing matches 2011 baseline)
   - Jacobite (was UDF historically, brief LDF wobble in 2021, +9.4 swing matches 2011 levels)

2. **LDF-historically-aligned cohorts that flipped in 2026**:
   - Latin Catholic (LDF-leaning 2011-2021, +9.0 swing flipped to UDF)
   - Marthoma (LDF wins in 2016+2021, UDF wins in 2026 after +7.1 swing)
   - These are the genuine "shifts" — not returns to baseline

3. **Perennial swing**:
   - Indian Orthodox (always close to 50/50, won split 5/4/1 in 2026)

4. **Trivandrum-district cluster (not really a denomination cohort)**:
   - CSI 5 ACs — geographically confounded, finding belongs in a "what the data can't tell us" appendix

5. **Sample-too-small**:
   - Marthoma n=2 — directionally interesting but no statistical claim

### So the cleaner reading is:

**Two structurally different 2026 stories within Christian Kerala**:
- **Catholic / Jacobite cohorts**: returned to long-run UDF baselines after 2-cycle LDF wobble. Continuity story.
- **Latin Catholic / Marthoma / Below-threshold cohorts**: genuinely flipped against historical LDF/competitive pattern. Shift story.

The Indian Orthodox cohort is a third type — it didn't flip, didn't return, just stayed close, with UDF nudging slightly ahead.

### Open follow-ups for §7

- [ ] Validate the "return vs shift" framing — what was the 2006 / 2001 alliance pattern? If Latin Catholic was UDF-leaning in 2001, 2026 is also a "return".
- [ ] Within Latin Catholic, separate the genuinely-shifted ACs from the regression-to-baseline ACs.

---

## Synthesis candidates (running list)

What story might tie the sections together?

1. **"The 2026 Christian swing was actually two stories":** SM/Jacobite returned to baseline; Latin Catholic/Marthoma shifted. The headline 'Christians moved to UDF' obscures this.

2. **"Sub-rite identity is a *historical* axis, not a *2026* axis":** Sub-rite explains *which* baseline each cohort returned to or shifted from. It doesn't explain the *direction* of 2026 movement — that's mostly statewide anti-incumbency.

3. **"NDA's Christian-area wins are a Trivandrum-district story":** Both Christian-cohort NDA wins (Nemom, Kazhakkoottam) are TVM. North Kerala Latin is BJP-resistant. Sub-rite isn't the BJP-success variable; geography is.

4. **"KEC/UDF candidate allocation is Catholic-aligned":** Kerala Congress factions cluster in Catholic cohorts (esp. Syro-Malabar) but are absent from Protestant cohorts (CSI, Marthoma). The UDF "Christian alliance party" is functionally Catholic-only.

## Falsifiers / what would weaken the thesis

(updated as findings landed)

- ✅ **CSI cohort = Trivandrum cluster** — confirmed in §2. CSI denomination effect not separable from district effect.
- [ ] **Multi-cycle test for cohort R²**: if cohort R² for swing collapses when controlling for 2011→2021 trajectory, sub-rite identity is a 2026-specific artefact.
- [ ] **District-fixed-effect test**: SM cluster in Kottayam, Latin coastal. Need to partial out district to see if cohort effect survives.
- [ ] **Latin Catholic-only south vs north**: if the 9.0pp swing is concentrated in N Kerala Latin (BJP-absent) and weak in TVM Latin (BJP-strong), Latin Catholic isn't a uniform cohort.

## Open data needs / scripts to run

- [ ] **Within-Latin north/south swing split** — does the +9pp Latin Catholic swing differ between TVM/Kollam coastal vs Kannur/Kasaragod coastal?
- [ ] **2001/2006 Christian-cohort baseline** — was Latin LDF-leaning in 2001? Confirms or breaks the "return vs shift" framing
- [ ] **District-fixed-effect regression** — model UDF 2026 with cohort + district FE; does cohort survive?
- [ ] **NDA-target Christian ACs in 2026 vs 2021 vs 2016** — what's BJP's strategy in Christian ACs across cycles? Are the Trivandrum wins consolidating or static?
- [ ] **Twenty 20 attribution** — within the Jacobite +8.3pp NDA jump, how much is Twenty 20 vs BJP?

## Decisions log

- **2026-05-10 (initial)**: draft seeded with Sprint 2 findings. Working thesis = three Christian profiles.
- **2026-05-10 (revision after detail script)**: reframed around 7 question sections (Q1–Q7).
  - **CSI cohort is geographically confounded** (all 5 Trivandrum). The "CSI is LDF-leaning" framing dropped.
  - **Multi-cycle revealed Latin Catholic was structurally LDF-leaning** through 2011–2021. The +9pp swing is more like a flip/return than a structural shift.
  - **Twenty 20 didn't target Jacobite denomination** but accidentally inflated Jacobite-cohort NDA via Kunnathunad concentration.
  - **Latin Catholic + BJP claim** is directionally correct but geographically uneven (south Latin coast has BJP; north Latin doesn't).
  - **KEC allocation is Catholic-aligned**, with Syro-Malabar getting the most.
  - Provisional thesis revised to "sub-rite identity is a second-order historical-baseline variable, not a primary 2026-swing variable".
